added_rules.content: When writing stress tests, target a specific invariant (e.g. balance never negative, exactly-once) rather than generic load — invariant-first tests produce sharper assertions
After establishing a stress test pattern, reuse the seed-hammer-assert template for new concurrency tests — do not reinvent the structure each time
Always verify container image builds for workspace crates in CI — single-service build files break when workspace dependencies are outside the build context
Security audit cycle must follow fix-then-prove: every security fix bead gets a paired E2E test bead that validates at real HTTP boundary with real databases
When multiple stress tests run in the same sprint, serialize them via bead dependencies to avoid cargo build lock contention across agents
Keep retro analysis windows to 5-10 bead closes — larger windows produce diminishing insight and slow down the retro bead itself
Negative monetary validation must be tested at HTTP boundary in every money-handling module — never trust DB constraints alone for business rule enforcement
